controls.name: Broken Access Control
controls.description: Moving up from the fifth position, 94% of applications were tested for some form of broken access control. Access control enforces policy such that users cannot act outside of their intended permissions.

controls.name: Cryptographic Failures
controls.description: Previously known as Sensitive Data Exposure. Failures related to cryptography which often lead to exposure of sensitive data.

controls.name: Injection
controls.description: Injection flaws occur when untrusted data is sent to an interpreter as part of a command or query. Common injection types include SQL, NoSQL, OS command, LDAP, and expression language.

controls.name: Insecure Design
controls.description: New category for 2021 focusing on risks related to design and architectural flaws. Calls for more use of threat modeling, secure design patterns, and reference architectures.

controls.name: Security Misconfiguration
controls.description: Moving up from #6 in the previous edition, 90% of applications were tested for some form of misconfiguration. Includes insecure default configurations, incomplete configurations, open cloud storage, verbose error messages.

controls.name: Vulnerable and Outdated Components
controls.description: Previously titled Using Components with Known Vulnerabilities. You are likely vulnerable if you do not know the versions of all components you use or if the software is vulnerable, unsupported, or out of date.

controls.name: Identification and Authentication Failures
controls.description: Previously known as Broken Authentication. Confirmation of user's identity, authentication, and session management is critical to protect against authentication-related attacks.

controls.name: Software and Data Integrity Failures
controls.description: New category for 2021 focuses on making assumptions related to software updates, critical data, and CI/CD pipelines without verifying integrity.

controls.name: Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
controls.description: Previously known as Insufficient Logging & Monitoring. Without logging and monitoring, breaches cannot be detected. Insufficient logging, detection, monitoring and active response occurs any time.
references: OWASP ASVS V7 Error Handling and Logging

controls.name: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
controls.description: New category for 2021. SSRF flaws occur whenever a web application is fetching a remote resource without validating the user-supplied URL.
